title: howl / lust howls
body:
# howl / lust howls

A recurring word in Colin's track titles across the alphabets era. Two
overlapping uses: the specific phrase **"lust howls"** (a near-direct
restatement that surfaces three times across six years), and a broader
**"howl"** vocabulary attached to animals, dreams, and calls.

This is the first motif documented in the vault and serves as the worked
example for the `motif` kind.

## Tracks (chronological)

### "lust" + "howl" cluster

- `_Documentation/Music/alphabets/07_08 [_]/lust howls in the afternoon.mp3` _(2007–2008)_
- `_Documentation/Music/alphabets/07_08 [_]/lust howls and calls for mating like locusts do.m4a` _(2007–2008)_
- `_Documentation/Music/alphabets/09 [_]/lust calls and sex dance.m4a` _(2009; "lust calls" variant — same conceptual register)_
- `_Documentation/Music/alphabets/unreleased/lust howls.mp3` _(2013; Ableton .asd sidecar from 2015 indicates Colin returned to the file)_

### Broader "howl" vocabulary

- `_Documentation/Music/alphabets/BESTUV09/11 plesiosaur howl outs and swim bump.mp3` _(2009)_
- `_Documentation/Music/alphabets/sep09/04 plesiosaur howl outs and swim bump.mp3` _(2009 — likely same source)_
- `_Documentation/Music/alphabets/fantazia2010/15 robo goats howl and bang.mp3` _(2010)_
- `_Documentation/Music/alphabets/DEC09SCIFI/04 robodogs nighttime cage howls.mp3` _(2009)_
- `_Documentation/Music/alphabets/M_A_Y_09[DONTTAKEITSERIOUS,IMALITTLEKID]/12 maywolves hum and talk w_ shooting stars in the background.m4a` _(May 2009 — "maywolves" implies the same animal-howling register)_

## Reading

Across six years, "lust howls" / "howl" sits at the intersection of three
registers Colin used: **animal calls** (locusts, plesiosaurs, robo goats,
robodogs, maywolves), **erotic/bodily language** ("lust", "calls for
mating", "sex dance"), and **late-night dream/ritual energy** (the
`unreleased/` 2013 track sits next to "DEC09 SCIFI" 2009 framing).

The phrase isn't loud — it appears in titles, not band names or release
names — but it's persistent. For a website, this is the kind of motif page
that lets a reader follow a thread through Colin's vocabulary that the
project/release structure doesn't expose.

## Notes for future passes

- The 2015 Ableton `.asd` sidecar on `unreleased/lust howls.mp3` suggests
  Colin returned to the track that year. Worth checking adjacent 2015-era
  Killd By session files for any "howl" references; the motif may have
  carried into Killd By that the alphabets-only sweep missed.
- "calls" as a word might be a sister-motif (lust calls, plesiosaur howl outs
  and... swim bump) — possibly worth its own entry if a sweep finds 5+ tracks.
